description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: test and deploy, using the mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/sdk:2.2.301 Docker image as default. In the test stage add a manual code_quality job (skipped if $CODE_QUALITY_DISABLED) that uses docker:latest with a docker:dind service, sets DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay, runs 'docker pull codeclimate/codeclimate' and then 'docker run --env CODECLIMATE_DEBUG=1 --env CODECLIMATE_CODE="$PWD" --volume "$PWD":/code --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock --volume /tmp/cc:/tmp/cc codeclimate/codeclimate analyze -f html > report.html', and stores report.html as an artifact. Also in the test stage add a debug job that runs 'echo | dotnet --version' and 'dotnet tool install dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool --tool-path tools' in before_script, then executes 'dotnet test --logger "junit" --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage"', lists test result directories, generates text summary and HTML coverage reports with './tools/reportgenerator' commands, lists and cats the summary, and finally echoes "End Summary"; it publishes the Reports_Coverage/ directory as an artifact, includes JUnit reports matching "*.Test/TestResults/TestResults.xml", and captures coverage using the regex /Line coverage[\s\S].+%/. In the deploy stage add a release job that runs only on the master branch, executes 'dotnet publish -c Release -o ../publish MyProject/MyProject.csproj', and stores the publish/ folder as an artifact.
